article_title: Capcom Reveals Resident Evil Veronica, a 2027 Remake of the 2000 Dreamcast Game Code Veronica, for PS5, Xbox, Switch 2, and PC

content_preview.title: Capcom Reveals Resident Evil Veronica, a 2027 Remake of the 2000 Dreamcast Game Code Veronica, for PS5, Xbox, Switch 2, and PC
content_preview.summary: Capcom unveiled Resident Evil Veronica at Summer Game Fest 2026, a remake of 2000's Code Veronica due in 2027, with no gameplay shown.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Capcom used Summer Game Fest 2026 to announce Resident Evil Veronica, a remake of the 2000 survival-horror title Resident Evil Code: Veronica. According to [Video Games Chronicle](https://www.videogameschronicle.com/news/resident-evil-code-veronica-is-officially-the-next-game-in-the-series-to-get-a-remake/), the game is officially titled Resident Evil Veronica and is scheduled to launch in 2027. Capcom described the project alongside its reveal trailer, saying: "This new title prese...

editor_notes.content_quality: Tightly structured News piece (448 words, within the 400-1200 News range). Clear sectioning (Overview, What We Know, The Producer's Case, What We Don't Know). Every paragraph attributes its claims to a named, linked source.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 source snapshots read from disk and sha256-verified against the manifest (source-0/1/2 all match). source-0 (videogameschronicle.com news, allowlisted) confirms: official title 'Resident Evil Veronica', remake of 2000's 'Resident Evil Code: Veronica', 2027 release on PS5/Xbox Series X/S/Switch 2/PC, Dreamcast exclusive, Claire and Chris Redfield surviving an outbreak on a remote prison island, and the Capcom official-description quote verbatim ('This new title preserves the essence of the original game, while introducing modernized gameplay, a reimagined storyline, and vividly detailed graphics'). source-1 (videogameschronicle.com producer feature, allowlisted) confirms producer Yoshiaki Hirabayashi and all four attributed quotes verbatim (the 'just as vital and important' quote, the 'one-word subtitle' quote, the 'Claire after the events in Raccoon City' quote plus 'her experiences haven't left her the exact same person that she was in RE2', and 'a third-person game'). source-2 (kotaku.com, allowlisted) confirms Rockfort Island, events three months after Resident Evil 2, the same team that made the RE2 and RE4 remakes, and third-person only with no first-person toggle. GameSpot/Eurogamer/IGN are not cited.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: No hallucinations detected. CRITICAL CHECK PASSED: the primary source states the release year as '2027' with no 'Q1'/'first quarter' qualifier anywhere in the snapshot; the article correctly uses '2027' and does not invent a 'Q1 2027' window (Rule 2 ambiguity resolved in favor of the primary source). Proper nouns spelled verbatim per source: 'Code Veronica'/'Code: Veronica', 'Rockfort Island', 'Hirabayashi' (Rule 7). The 'no gameplay shown' framing is supported (source-1: producer 'isn't revealing many gameplay details'; reveal was a trailer; source-2: Capcom 'trying to reveal as little as possible').
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. No sensationalism, no editorializing, no AI self-reference. 'What We Don't Know' section honestly bounds the reveal.
editor_notes.originality: New topic. No prior Resident Evil Veronica / Code Veronica coverage in the archive. Adjacent June 2026 gaming coverage (Street Fighter 6 milestone, Sony State of Play) covers distinct events with no overlap of facts, sources, or angle.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Clean, accurate, well-sourced News submission. Every direct quote is verbatim from a cited, allowlisted source; every specific (year, platforms, names, setting, dev team) traces to a snapshot; headline, summary, and lead are all source-backed; no orphan source URLs. The standout risk on this story — fabricating a 'Q1 2027' date — was avoided. Verdict: APPROVE.
